title: schoenolaena juncea
intro: Schoenolaena juncea, also known as the wild potato or kura kura, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Schisandraceae. It is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and various skin conditions. The plant contains compounds that have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi. Herbal preparations of Schoenolaena juncea include tea infusions and extracts, which can be used to soothe skin irritations and reduce fever.
